title: Knowledge Webhook Events

story.i_want_to: receive webhook notifications when knowledge articles are created, updated, or linked
story.so_that: external dashboards and tools can react to knowledge wiki changes in real-time

rationale: The webhook system (Epic 10) provides push-based event delivery to external systems. Extending it to cover knowledge wiki events enables external tools to react in real-time when new patterns are documented, conventions are updated, or articles are superseded. This is especially valuable for AI orchestration dashboards that need to track knowledge accumulation metrics, and for notification systems that alert team members when important decisions or findings are recorded. Reusing the existing EventGenerator infrastructure keeps the implementation minimal.

acceptance_criteria.description: EventGenerator.generate_events/3 is called in the Knowledge context after article creation, producing an 'article.created' event with payload including article id, title, category, project_id, and status.
EventGenerator.generate_events/3 is called after article update, producing an 'article.updated' event with payload including article id, title, category, project_id, changed fields, and new status.
EventGenerator.generate_events/3 is called after article archival, producing an 'article.archived' event with payload including article id, title, and category.

When an article's status is set to :superseded (via a supersedes link), an 'article.superseded' event is generated with payload including the superseded article id and title, plus the superseding article id.

EventGenerator.generate_events/3 is called after article link creation, producing an 'article_link.created' event with payload including link id, source_article_id, target_article_id, relationship_type, and both article titles.

EventGenerator.generate_events/3 is called after article link deletion, producing an 'article_link.deleted' event with payload including link id, source_article_id, target_article_id, and relationship_type.

The new event types (article.created, article.updated, article.archived, article.superseded, article_link.created, article_link.deleted) are added to the list of allowed webhook subscription event types, so tenants can subscribe to them.
Events are generated within the same Ecto.Multi transaction as the entity mutation, ensuring events are only created when the mutation succeeds.
Event payloads include tenant_id for routing and the article's project_id for project-scoped webhook filtering.

test_cases.name: Article creation generates article.created event
expected_results: A webhook_event record is created with event_type 'article.created'
Event payload includes article id, title='New Pattern', category='pattern', project_id=nil

test_cases.name: Article update generates article.updated event
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: "Old Title"}) as article
expected_results: A webhook_event record is created with event_type 'article.updated'
Event payload includes article id and new title

test_cases.name: Supersedes link generates both article_link.created and article.superseded events
steps: Call Knowledge.create_link(tenant.id, %{source_article_id: new_article.id, target_article_id: old_article.id, relationship_type: :supersedes})
expected_results: A webhook_event with event_type 'article_link.created' is created
A webhook_event with event_type 'article.superseded' is created
The superseded event payload includes both the superseded and superseding article ids

test_cases.name: Article archival generates article.archived event
expected_results: A webhook_event record is created with event_type 'article.archived'
Event payload includes article id, title, and category

test_cases.name: Link deletion generates article_link.deleted event
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, source_article_id: article_a.id, target_article_id: article_b.id}) as link
expected_results: A webhook_event record is created with event_type 'article_link.deleted'
Event payload includes link id, source_article_id, target_article_id, and relationship_type

test_cases.name: New event types are valid for webhook subscriptions
expected_results: All six knowledge event types are accepted as valid webhook events

technical_notes: Wire events into the existing Knowledge context mutations (US-19.3). Add EventGenerator.generate_events/3 calls within the Ecto.Multi pipelines.

Event generation pattern: `Multi.new() |> Multi.insert(:article, changeset) |> Audit.log_in_multi(:article, "article.created") |> Multi.run(:events, fn _repo, %{article: article} -> EventGenerator.generate_events(tenant_id, "article.created", article) end)`.
Add the six new event types to the known event types list used for webhook subscription validation (defined in US-10.1). This is likely a module attribute or constant list in the Webhooks context.
The article.superseded event is generated as a side-effect of the supersedes link creation (US-19.5 AC-19.5.4). It should be generated in the same Multi transaction.
Event payloads should include tenant_id and project_id for webhook routing and filtering. The existing webhook delivery system uses project_id to match project-scoped webhook subscriptions.
Config-based DI: EventGenerator is likely accessed via a behaviour. Check the existing webhook infrastructure for the correct DI pattern and mock in config/test.exs.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs in DataCase/ConnCase setup, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex, (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
